APEUSDT.P.bias: Fresh impulsive rally off the 0.1345 higher-low is pushing directly into the old 0.150-0.152 range top (this week's prior resistance). Do not chase into the level; only a confirmed reclaim-and-hold, followed by a retest that holds, is tradable.

APEUSDT.P.setup_type: Breaker / Failed Supply-Demand Flip / Compression-to-Expansion Breakout

entry.basis: Deep retest of the 0.1345 higher-low / rally launch zone, only after 0.1520 reclaims and holds. Requires a rejection/confirmation candle inside the zone, not a blind limit.

APEUSDT.P.invalidation: 1H acceptance below 0.1295, or a failed reclaim that closes back below 0.1520 without ever holding.
APEUSDT.P.nearest_liquidity: Buy-side above 0.1520 then 0.1580 (untested spike high) and 0.1620/0.1700; sell-side below 0.1345 then 0.1295 and the deeper 4H base near 0.1260.
APEUSDT.P.reclaim_status: Price is testing 0.1520 (old range top) right now; not yet confirmed as reclaimed and held.
APEUSDT.P.relative_strength: Strong intraday (best performer on the visible watchlist while BTC/ETH are red), but this is a bounce within a multi-month downtrend on the daily chart, not a structural trend reversal.
APEUSDT.P.notes: 2026-07-08 first analysis. Daily chart shows a multi-month downtrend from ~0.65 to sub-0.10 lows with a blow-off spike to 0.33 in May that fully retraced; current bounce is counter-trend within that structure. Shallow retest into 0.1440-0.1470 fails the intraday R:R floor (~1.3R to first liquidity) and should be skipped even if it happens; only the deep 0.1345-0.1375 zone clears 2R to TP1. If price never returns there, this is MISSED / NO CHASE, not a thesis loss.

journal.result: Chart's 'AutoTrader Trade Map' script source was corrupted to a garbage string, with two stacked broken instances on the chart. Not a trading mistake; APE's setup thesis was unaffected and rendered correctly once fixed.
journal.note: Recovery: reload the chart tab, reopen Pine Editor, select-all + paste correct script via a real paste event (clipboard write + native paste, or a synthetic ClipboardEvent dispatched on the Monaco .inputarea if OS clipboard permission is unavailable), save, then explicitly click Add to chart (saving alone does not reattach a removed/broken instance). Verify by reading the rendered card, not by absence of the compile-error icon alone.
